implementationArtifact.specBody:
---
complexity: low
---

# Feature: Add Footer

## Summary

Add a simple footer to the bottom of every page with copyright text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Footer appears at the bottom of the page
- [ ] Footer contains copyright text with the current year and "BrewBoard"

## Technical Approach

Add a footer element to `src/app/page.tsx`. One line, no complex styling needed.

## Out of Scope

- Do NOT write tests
- Do NOT add documentation
- Do NOT refactor existing code

## Validation

```bash
echo "Feature Add Footer validated"
```

quality.summary: The implementation captures the right intent — a footer with copyright text and correct year — but introduces a fatal JSX error: `<main>` and `<footer>` are returned as adjacent sibling elements without a Fragment wrapper. TypeScript will reject this with 'JSX expressions must have one parent element', so the app does not build. The fix is trivial (add `<>…</>`) but it was not applied. Content, styling choices, and minimality are all solid; the structural mistake is the only issue but it is a showstopper.

requirements.notes: Both acceptance criteria are addressed in intent: footer is placed after `<main>`, contains '© 2026 BrewBoard'. However, because the implementation fails to compile, neither criterion is actually satisfied at runtime. Deducting for the broken output despite correct intent.

correctness.notes: Definitive JSX error confirmed by reading the file. Lines 24 (`</main>`) and 25 (`<footer>`) are adjacent root-level siblings inside a single `return (…)`. React/TypeScript requires one root element; this triggers 'JSX expressions must have one parent element' at compile time. A `<>…</>` Fragment wrapper around both elements would fix it in one line.

minimality.notes: Change is tightly scoped: 3 lines added to `page.tsx`, plus a standard (mostly empty) implementation log. No refactoring, no new dependencies, no extraneous changes.

code_quality.notes: Tailwind classes (`w-full text-center p-4 text-stone-500`) are consistent with the existing style vocabulary. `©` is correct JSX. Minor issues: interior indentation of the footer (4-space body vs 2-space outer) is slightly inconsistent with surrounding code; year is hardcoded to 2026 rather than `new Date().getFullYear()`. Structural error overshadows otherwise fine choices.

risk.notes: The JSX compilation failure means the entire app is broken — not a degraded UX, but a hard build failure. Risk score is accordingly low. The fix is low-effort but was not applied.
